7.2. Learned senior counsel submits that principles of natural justice 
cannot be applied as a straight jacket formula. NGT was careful 
enough to direct the joint committee to furnish copies of its 
reports to the project proponent (appellant). Appellant was, 
therefore, very much aware of the contents of the two reports, 
yet it did not contest the same before the NGT. That being the 
308 
Supreme Court Reports
position, the contentions advanced on behalf of the appellant 
are without any substance. 
7.3. He finally submits that the civil appeals being devoid of merit 
are, therefore, liable to be dismissed. 
8. 
Submissions made by learned counsel for the parties have received 
the due consideration of the Court. 
9. 
At the outset, it would be apposite to refer to some of the relevant 
statutory provisions.
